title: ranunculus villosus subsp. constantinopolitanus
intro: Ranunculus villosus subsp. constantinopolitanus, also known as the Constantine clematis or Constantinople buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the ranunculus family, characterized by its delicate white or pale yellow flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Ranunculus villosus subsp. constantinopolitanus include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall skin health.
